headline: Qualcomm (QCOM) Stock Is Up, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of wireless chipmaker Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) jumped 3.4% in the afternoon session after President Trump announced Apple had agreed to design and manufacture chips with Intel in the United States, a foundry validation the market had been waiting on for over a year.

headline: Applied Materials (AMAT) Launches SENZ With Qualcomm And EssilorLuxottica For AI Eyewear
summary: Applied Materials has launched SENZ, an integrated ambient visual system for AI-powered smart eyewear. The platform was co-developed with EssilorLuxottica, GlobalFoundries, and Qualcomm to support next generation AR glasses. The announcement signals an expansion of Applied Materials' role beyond semiconductor equipment into consumer optical technologies. headline: QUALCOMM (QCOM) Is Up 11.4% After Launching Custom AI Data Center Silicon With a Hyperscaler
summary: In recent weeks, Qualcomm has moved beyond its smartphone roots by launching a custom AI data center silicon program with a leading hyperscaler, expanding collaborations in XR, edge AI, and AI infrastructure across partners like Nuvoton, SLB, ByteDance, and Primax Tymphany. This broad push into custom data center chips and over 40 new AI-powered device designs signals a meaningful reshaping of Qualcomm’s role across cloud, personal devices, and industrial applications. headline: Qualcomm (QCOM) Is Reportedly Eyeing Tenstorrent In A $10 Billion AI Chip Deal
summary: Qualcomm, ticker NasdaqGS:QCOM, is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent. The potential deal is valued at around US$8b to US$10b. The transaction, if completed, would extend Qualcomm’s reach beyond smartphones into AI and data center hardware. Qualcomm is widely known for its smartphone processors, but the reported move for Tenstorrent points to a broader ambition around AI computing and data center hardware. For you as an investor, this brings the discussion...

headline: Nuvoton Technology Corporation Strengthens Tethered XR Glass SoC Business Through Collaboration With Qualcomm Technologies
summary: Nuvoton Technology Corporation announced its collaboration with Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. to strengthen its system-on-chip (SoC) business for tethered XR glass applications. By combining Nuvoton's low-power, XR-optimized SoCs with Qualcomm's Snapdragon® XR platforms, the companies aim to accelerate development, improve system performance, and expand XR adoption across industrial and enterprise markets.
